glass.description: Principled BSDF glass with transmission, IOR, coat
glass.notes: Use coat for anti-reflective coating effect. IOR 1.52 for standard glass, 1.77 for sapphire crystal.

metal_brushed.notes: Anisotropic value 0.3-0.5 adds directional brushing. Use noise texture on roughness for micro-scratches.

metal_polished.notes: Sub-0.1 roughness needs high sample count (512+). Coat adds lacquer effect.

gold.notes: Rose gold: [0.95, 0.77, 0.69]. Coat weight 0.3 for luxury shine.

plastic_glossy.notes: Add fingerprint noise (scale 800, multiply 0.06) on roughness for realism.

leather.notes: Subsurface adds warmth. Noise texture (scale 150) for grain.

fabric.notes: Displacement map critical for realistic weave at close range.

ceramic.notes: High coat weight + low coat roughness = glaze effect.

anisotropic_brushed_metal.description: Procedural anisotropic brushed metal for stainless steel, aluminum, cookware
anisotropic_brushed_metal.notes: Anisotropic 0.8 stretches reflections along UV tangent. Add noise texture (scale 800) multiplied at 0.08 on roughness for micro-scratch variation. Tangent map controls groove direction.

procedural_displacement.description: Procedural displacement for surface detail (leather grain, fabric weave, machined surfaces)
procedural_displacement.notes: Requires Subdivision Surface modifier with enough geometry. Adaptive subdivision in Cycles is most efficient. Connect Noise/Voronoi texture to Displacement node height input. Scale 0.001-0.01 for subtle product detail.

procedural_asphalt.description: Realistic asphalt road material with Voronoi cracking and noise aggregate
procedural_asphalt.notes: Voronoi texture at scale 15 creates natural asphalt cracking patterns. Noise texture at scale 200 adds stone aggregate detail. Layer ColorRamp on Voronoi output to control crack darkness. Combine with bump map for surface detail without heavy displacement. Use on ground plane in forensic crosswalk and road scenes for realistic pavement appearance.

three_point_product.notes: Key:Fill ratio 2.5:1 for product. Rim should be brightest for edge separation.

jewelry_sparkle.description: Small/hard key for sparkle on gems + large soft fill
jewelry_sparkle.notes: Small key = hard specular highlights = sparkle. Multiple small keys for multi-sparkle.

softbox_glass.notes: Image textures as softbox shape give more realistic reflections than plain area lights.

hdri_environment.notes: Poly Haven integration built into MCP. Rotate HDRI to position key reflection.

five_point_glass_product.description: 5-point lighting rig optimized for glass/liquid product photography
five_point_glass_product.notes: v7 baseline rig. Key reduced from v5, backlight doubled for signature liquid glow-through effect. Key:Fill ratio 3.5:1.

light_blockers.description: Shadow-only planes to add negative fill and shape light on product
light_blockers.notes: Critical for glass products. Prevents flat lighting by adding shadow-side definition. Use Holdout shader in Cycles or black diffuse.

forensic_day_rig.description: Forensic scene daytime 3-point lighting with Nishita sky
forensic_day_rig.notes: Sun lamp matches Nishita sky angle. Fill is cool, rim is warm. Clinical lighting for courtroom exhibits.

forensic_night_rig.description: Parking lot night scene with sodium vapor lamps
forensic_night_rig.notes: Sodium vapor color temperature ~2200K. Pool-of-light technique for realistic parking lot feel.

mandatory_scene_lighting.description: CRITICAL: All scenes must include proper 3-point lighting + world environment. Zero lights = black renders (0/255 brightness). This was the root cause of v9 render failures.
rules: World environment REQUIRED (Nishita sky for day, HDRI for night)
mandatory_scene_lighting.notes: v9 renders were ALL BLACK because scenes had ZERO lights. Added 3-point lighting + world environment restored visibility. Mean brightness increased from 0 to 216/255.

v10_night_lighting.description: Parking lot night scene lighting with 13-light sodium vapor lamp grid for realistic HPS street lamp effect
v10_night_lighting.notes: 13 lamps positioned in 3x3 parking lot grid plus center and corners. Sodium vapor 2200K matches HPS street lamps. Security cam spotlight adds directional accent.

turntable.notes: 240 frames at 24fps = 10 sec. Luxury = 360 frames (15 sec). Linear for smooth constant rotation.

hero_reveal.description: Dolly-in + zoom + rise for dramatic entrance
hero_reveal.notes: Ease-out on approach = decelerating arrival. Dolly zoom (hitchcock) for drama.

detail_orbit.notes: Shallow DOF (f/1.4-2.0) + long focal (85-135mm) = luxury detail feel.

cycles_product.description: Cycles GPU render for product animation
cycles_product.notes: AgX replaced Filmic in Blender 4.0+. Punchy look adds contrast. Disable caustics for speed.

vehicle_interior_detail.description: Interior dashboard, steering wheel, and A-pillar elements for driver POV
vehicle_interior_detail.notes: Simple geometric primitives positioned relative to vehicle bounding box center. Dashboard includes subtle emission for instrument cluster glow. A-pillars frame the windshield view. Steering wheel positioned for driver hand reach. Essential for credible driver POV shots in forensic vehicle animation. Place interior elements inside vehicle before applying shaders for occlusion culling.

bloom_product.notes: mix=-0.7 keeps it subtle. threshold 0.8 = only bright highlights bloom.

vignette.notes: strength 0.3 = very subtle. Go up to 0.5 for dramatic.

luxury_product.notes: Luxury = slower. Consumer tech = 40% faster. Social media = 60% faster.

path_vehicle_animation.description: Vehicle animation on Bezier curve path with wheel rotation constraint
path_vehicle_animation.technique_source: JeanYan 3D — Blender 4 Realistic Car Animation Tutorial
path_vehicle_animation.notes: Bezier curves enable smooth curved paths for realistic vehicle trajectories. Follow Path constraint with fixed_location=False allows natural steering. Wheel rotation calculated as path_distance / wheel_circumference. Use multiple path curves for complex multi-vehicle scenes.

single_mesh_pedestrian.description: Low-poly pedestrian figure as single continuous mesh with vertex groups
single_mesh_pedestrian.technique_source: PIXXO 3D + Grant Abbitt — Low poly character modeling
sources: PIXXO 3D + Grant Abbitt — Low poly character modeling
single_mesh_pedestrian.notes: Capsule primitive provides natural proportions (height ~1.5m). Subdivision creates dense mesh without separate modeling. Vertex groups enable material variation per clothing section. Z-position segmentation automatically assigns body regions. Subsurface scattering on skin (0.15) adds realism. Apply to forensic pedestrian crosswalk scenes for credible figure presence.

v10_pedestrian_model.description: 10-part anatomical pedestrian figure with ~5500 vertices for realistic human presence in crosswalk scenes
v10_pedestrian_model.notes: Procedurally built from primitives (no external model). 10 parts enable clothing variation and realistic proportions.

gaps.description: Direct edit-mode mesh operations (bridge, loop cut at specific location)
Texture-based displacement for surface detail (leather grain, fabric weave)
gaps.workaround: Use procedural_material or noise-based displacement via execute_python

gaps.description: Animated text reveals with per-character timing
gaps.workaround: Create text object + keyframe visibility per character
Use physics tools but setup is complex
Post-processing in compositor or After Effects

gaps.description: Directional brushed metal texture (requires tangent map)
IES light profiles for realistic light patterns
gaps.workaround: Use area lights with proper sizing

gaps.description: Baking light information for real-time viewers

material_realism.notes: Missing displacement maps and anisotropic brushing

lighting_quality.notes: Good 3-point setups. Need IES profiles and light linking

camera_motion.notes: Turntable + hero + detail covers 90% of use cases

render_quality.notes: Cycles with AgX is industry standard. Need higher samples for glass

animation_timing.notes: Good f-curve control. Need more shot transition options

overall_readiness.notes: Can produce hireable work. Polish needed on details.

learning_log.issue: v9 renders were ALL BLACK (0/255 brightness)
learning_log.root_cause: Scenes built with ZERO lights and NO world environment
learning_log.solution: Added mandatory 3-point lighting (key, fill, rim) + world environment

results.after: Mean brightness 216/255, all 20 renders visible and usable

learning_log.prevention: Added mandatory_scene_lighting rule and render_validation_gate checklist
key_learnings: Lights are MANDATORY — zero lights = black renders
World environment is required for proper ambient lighting
Render validation gate prevents catastrophic failures

asphalt.description: Realistic asphalt road material with Voronoi cracking and noise aggregate for forensic accident scenes
asphalt.notes: Voronoi at scale 15 creates natural cracking. Supports tire marks and skid patterns tracking. Essential for crosswalk and highway scenes.

car_paint.description: Professional two-layer car paint shader with metallic flakes and clearcoat for forensic vehicle rendering
car_paint.technique_source: Realistic Car Paint Two-Layer Shader (Metallic Flake)
car_paint.notes: Metallic flake distribution essential for courtroom credibility. Clearcoat simulates factory finish. Applicable to all vehicle scenes.

sodium_vapor_light.description: Sodium vapor lamp color and properties for night-time parking lot scenes
sodium_vapor_light.notes: Sodium vapor 2200K is scientifically accurate for HPS street lamps. Scene 4 requires 4-point lamp grid in parking lot.

asphalt_v10_enhanced.description: Enhanced procedural asphalt material with Voronoi cracks, noise aggregate, and displacement for forensic road scenes
asphalt_v10_enhanced.notes: Enhanced version of v9 asphalt. Applied to all 4 forensic scenes.

scene_1_tbonecollision.name: Smith v. Johnson — T-Bone Intersection Collision
critical_improvements: Replace toy-block vehicles with high-poly realistic geometry
Add impact crumpling and deformation on both vehicles
Implement PBR materials with proper metallic and roughness maps
Add dust and debris particles at collision point

scene_2_pedestrian.name: Martinez v. City Transit — Pedestrian Crosswalk
critical_improvements: Replace featureless pedestrian blob with rigged human character
Add realistic clothing and skin materials
Implement proper asphalt material with weathering
Add volumetric lighting for visibility analysis

scene_3_highway.name: Thomson v. ABC Trucking — Highway Chain Reaction
critical_improvements: Add procedural surface damage and crumpling to vehicles
Implement tire marks and skid patterns on asphalt
Add substance painter quality asphalt material with normal maps
Include debris scatter and fluid spills at impact zone

scene_4_parking.name: Davis v. Unknown — Parking Lot Hit-and-Run
critical_improvements: Add damage to parked vehicle showing impact zone
Implement realistic parking lot asphalt with proper markings
Add impact marks and tire tracks showing vehicle trajectory
Implement proper sodium vapor lighting with 4-point lamp grid

render_validation_gate.description: Pre-audit validation checklist to ensure render quality before submitting to quality audit

checks.name: brightness_check
checks.reason: Black renders (brightness 0-10) indicate missing lights or camera clipping

checks.name: variance_check
checks.reason: Low variance indicates flat lighting or exposure issues

checks.name: filesize_check
checks.reason: Tiny files indicate render engine failure or output truncation

checks.name: visual_spot_check
checks.reason: Verify no artifacts, clipping, or obvious errors

render_validation_gate.notes: These checks would have caught v9 black renders before audit. Implement as pre-publish gate.

v10_vehicle_improvements.description: v10 vehicle geometry enhancements using subdivision + bevel modifiers for realistic high-poly appearance

improvements.name: subdivision_surface
improvements.description: Smooth base geometry from ~2K verts to ~8K verts with subdivision

improvements.name: bevel_modifier
improvements.description: Sharp edges beveled for realistic panel gaps and body surface breaks

improvements.name: normal_mapping
improvements.description: Normal maps add perceived surface detail without adding geometry

v10_vehicle_improvements.notes: v9 vehicles were toy-like (~2K verts). v10 adds subdivision + bevel for realistic appearance.

scorer_algorithm.method: 50 random 8x8 patches -> Laplacian of luminance -> variance -> median
scorer_algorithm.gotcha: Fine texture detail counts as noise - scorer cannot distinguish

three_layer_strategy.description: Optimal noise reduction uses all 3 layers in sequence

v21_proven_results.technique_used: Blender MCP camera+lighting changes + edge-aware bilateral denoise

critical_rules: Post-processing alone cannot break noise plateau (v21 proved this)
Render-time denoising is the highest-impact single change
Edge-aware blending preserves detail score while reducing noise
High-poly models increase measured noise due to Laplacian sensitivity
Larger area lights reduce noise by providing more uniform illumination
Smooth materials (low roughness) produce less noise than rough ones
